{{Short description|Modern metric system of measurement}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = International System of Units
| image = SI base units.svg
| caption = The seven [[SI base unit]]s and their interdependencies.
| topics = [[Physical constant]], [[Metre]], [[Kilogram]], [[Second]]
| abbreviation = SI
}}

The '''International System of Units''', internationally known by the abbreviation '''SI''' (from the French ''Système international d'unités''), is the modern form of the [[metric system]] and the world's most widely used [[system of measurement]]. It is the only system of measurement with official status in nearly every country in the world.

== SI base units ==
The SI comprises seven [[SI base unit|base units]]:

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Quantity !! Unit !! Symbol
|-
| [[Time]] || [[second]] || s
|-
| [[Length]] || [[metre]] || m
|-
| [[Mass]] || [[kilogram]] || kg
|-
| [[Electric current]] || [[ampere]] || A
|-
| [[Temperature]] || [[kelvin]] || K
|-
| [[Amount of substance]] || [[mole (unit)|mole]] || mol
|-
| [[Luminous intensity]] || [[candela]] || cd
|}

All other SI units are derived from these seven base units.

== 2019 redefinition ==
{{Main|2019 redefinition of the SI base units}}
Since 20 May 2019, all SI base units are defined in terms of fundamental [[physical constant]]s:

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Constant !! Defines !! Exact value
|-
| [[Speed of light]] <math>c</math> || metre || 299,792,458 m/s
|-
| [[Planck constant]] <math>h</math> || kilogram || 6.62607015×10⁻³⁴ J·s
|-
| [[Elementary charge]] <math>e</math> || ampere || 1.602176634×10⁻¹⁹ C
|-
| [[Boltzmann constant]] <math>k_B</math> || kelvin || 1.380649×10⁻²³ J/K
|-
| [[Avogadro constant]] <math>N_A</math> || mole || 6.02214076×10²³ mol⁻¹
|}

== Derived units ==
Common [[SI derived unit]]s:
* [[Hertz]] (Hz) — frequency
* [[Newton (unit)|Newton]] (N) — force
* [[Pascal (unit)|Pascal]] (Pa) — pressure
* [[Joule]] (J) — energy
* [[Watt]] (W) — power
* [[Coulomb]] (C) — electric charge
* [[Volt]] (V) — voltage
* [[Ohm]] (Ω) — resistance
* [[Tesla (unit)|Tesla]] (T) — magnetic flux density

== SI prefixes ==
The SI uses standard [[metric prefix|prefixes]] for multiples and submultiples:
* kilo- (k) = 10³, mega- (M) = 10⁶, giga- (G) = 10⁹
* milli- (m) = 10⁻³, micro- (μ) = 10⁻⁶, nano- (n) = 10⁻⁹

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Kilogram]]
* [[Metre]]
* [[Newton (unit)]]
* [[Physical constant]]
* [[Second]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:International System of Units| ]]
[[Category:Systems of units]]
[[Category:Measurement]]
